note: See README.md in this folder — this is a good-faith structured summary, not a verbatim platform export. Replace/supplement with an actual export from claude.ai for full assignment compliance.

turns.content_summary: Uploaded the full Practical Assignment brief (Autocomplete Form Requirements, FR-01 through FR-05, test environment details, 6-task practical exercise, submission structure) and instructed: "Complete this assignment as mentioned".
Scaffolded a git-repo-style directory structure (docs/, tests/ui, tests/api, mock-server/, prompts/, transcript/) matching the assignment's required submission structure.

Built a local reference mock server (Express) and front-end (HTML/JS) implementing the documented HTML structure and FR-01 through FR-05, including a config endpoint to toggle prefix-match vs match-anywhere filtering, and dedicated malformed-response endpoints for negative API testing.
Set up package.json and playwright.config.js with separate 'ui' and 'api' projects, and a webServer block to auto-start the mock app for test runs.
Wrote the Page Object Model (AutocompleteFormPage.js) and the UI test spec covering Tab Navigation, Keyboard Interaction, Suggestion Filtering, Suggestion Selection, and Form Submission.

Wrote the FR-05 JSON Schema and the API test suite (Ajv-based schema validation, data type checks, BCP 47 locale check, suggestion_list correctness, 2+ negative cases against simulated malformed responses).

Installed dependencies via npm (registry.npmjs.org was reachable). Attempted 'npx playwright install chromium' — failed because the sandbox's network egress allowlist blocks cdn.playwright.dev, so the UI suite's browser binary could not be downloaded in this environment.
actions: npx playwright install chromium (failed: host not in allowlist)

turns.content_summary: Ran the API suite against the mock server. Two tests failed on first run. Diagnosed and fixed both: (1) a hardcoded future start_date that could be later than the server's real 'now' end_date, replaced with a relative timestamp; (2) a test asserting anywhere-mode suggestion_list behavior using an invalid partial-text submission that the server correctly rejected with 400, rewritten to use a valid full-suggestion submission. Re-ran: 11/11 passed.
Sanity-checked the mock server directly via curl (config, suggestions, submission endpoints) to confirm behavior independent of the test framework.

Wrote all documentation deliverables: requirement analysis (with explicit stated assumptions), top-10 risk-ranked test scenarios, FR-05 defect/discrepancy identification against the sample response, 12 detailed test cases, AI usage reflection (tools/areas/modifications/limitations, using the two real bugs found above as concrete modification examples), and an architecture discussion.
Wrote the top-level README with setup/run instructions, and saved the original assignment brief plus a prompt-usage note into prompts/, and a transcript note plus this file into transcript/.
Packaged the full repository as a zip file and presented it as the final deliverable.

Requested a senior QA review of the completed repository: re-examine the suggestion_list defect finding for over-claiming, tighten the timestamp explanation, add an Additional Quality Considerations section (accessibility/performance) to requirement analysis, add one more negative API test, review the UI suite for duplicated locators/manual assertions, expand the README and architecture discussion, rewrite the AI reflection to show iterative/critical use rather than one-shot generation, and provide a summary of every file changed with rationale.

Reclassified the suggestion_list finding in 3-defect-identification.md from a confirmed Critical defect to an explicitly-labeled ambiguity, with the FR-02/FR-04 reasoning spelled out and a concrete follow-up check identified; updated the corresponding cross-references in 1-requirement-analysis.md and 2-test-scenarios.md to match. Rewrote the start_date/end_date explanation to state plainly that the values are valid ISO-8601 timestamps expressed in the wrong timezone (UTC instead of IST), rather than a vaguer 'local time' framing.

Added an 'Additional Quality Considerations' section to the requirement analysis covering accessibility (keyboard-only navigation, focus visibility, label association, screen-reader compatibility) and performance (filtering latency, submission latency, suggestion-list scalability).

Added a dedicated malformed-timestamp endpoint to the mock server and a matching negative API test asserting Ajv's date-time format check catches it; added the corresponding TC-13 to the test-cases doc. Re-ran the API suite: 12/12 passed.

Reviewed the UI suite for duplicated locators and manual assertions. Standardized several ad hoc .length comparisons to Playwright's toHaveLength() for consistency, tightened one assertion from a loose toBeLessThan(3) to an exact toHaveLength(2) since the exact expected count was already known, and replaced a deprecated .type() call with .pressSequentially(). Confirmed the Page Object Model itself needed no changes and contains no assertions.

Restructured the README into explicit Prerequisites, Installation, Running the Mock Server, Running UI Tests, Running API Tests, Running Everything, Assumptions, and Limitations sections; updated test-case and test-count references throughout. Expanded the architecture discussion's mock-server rationale to explicitly state the assignment's URL is illustrative/placeholder rather than an accessible environment.

Rewrote the AI Reflection to demonstrate iterative, critical use rather than one-shot acceptance: added the suggestion_list over-claiming as a concrete 'Modifications Made' example, added a note about generic first-draft risk scenarios being rewritten for specificity, and added a second AI Limitations example (over-confident defect classification) alongside the original test-suite limitation.

Re-ran the full API suite and syntax-checked all modified JavaScript files to confirm no regressions, then re-packaged the repository as a zip file for delivery, along with a summary of every changed file and the rationale for each change.
